class="truncate">Transformers</span></a><!----> <!----><p>How to use MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M2.7 with Transformers:</p> <!----><!----> <pre># Use a pipeline as a high-level helper
from transformers import pipeline

pipe = pipeline("text-generation", model="MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M2.7", trust_remote_code=True)
messages = [
    {"role": "user", "content": "Who are you?"},
]
pipe(messages)</pre><!----><!----> <pre># Load model directly
from transformers import AutoTokenizer, AutoModelForCausalLM

tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained("MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M2.7", trust_remote_code=True)
model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained("MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M2.7", trust_remote_code=True)
messages = [
    {"role": "user", "content": "Who are you?"},
]
inputs = tokenizer.apply_chat_template(
	messages,
	add_generation_prompt=True,
	tokenize=True,
	return_dict=True,
	return_tensors="pt",
).to(model.device)

outputs = model.generate(**inputs, max_new_tokens=40)

<p><strong>MiniMax-M2.7</strong> is our first model deeply participating in its own evolution. M2.7 is capable of building complex agent harnesses and completing highly elaborate productivity tasks, leveraging Agent Teams, complex Skills, and dynamic tool search. For more details, see our <a href="https://www.minimax.io/news/minimax-m27-en" rel="nofollow">blog post</a>.</p>
<p align="center">
  <img width="100%" src="/MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M2.7/resolve/main/figures/benchmark_overview.png">
</p>

<h2 class="relative group flex items-baseline">
	<a id="model-self-evolution" class="block pr-1.5 text-lg md:absolute md:p-1.5 md:opacity-0 md:group-hover:opacity-100 md:right-full" href="#model-self-evolution" rel="nofollow">
		<span class="header-link"><svg class="text-gray-500 hover:text-black dark:hover:text-gray-200 w-4" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" aria-hidden="true" role="img" width="1em" height="1em" preserveAspectRatio="xMidYMid meet" viewBox="0 0 256 256"><path d="M167.594 88.393a8.001 8.001 0 0 1 0 11.314l-67.882 67.882a8 8 0 1 1-11.314-11.315l67.882-67.881a8.003 8.003 0 0 1 11.314 0zm-28.287 84.86l-28.284 28.284a40 40 0 0 1-56.567-56.567l28.284-28.284a8 8 0 0 0-11.315-11.315l-28.284 28.284a56 56 0 0 0 79.196 79.197l28.285-28.285a8 8 0 1 0-11.315-11.314zM212.852 43.14a56.002 56.002 0 0 0-79.196 0l-28.284 28.284a8 8 0 1 0 11.314 11.314l28.284-28.284a40 40 0 0 1 56.568 56.567l-28.285 28.285a8 8 0 0 0 11.315 11.314l28.284-28.284a56.065 56.065 0 0 0 0-79.196z" fill="currentColor"></path></svg></span>
	</a>
	<span>
		Model Self-Evolution
	</span>
</h2>
<p>M2.7 initiates a cycle of model self-evolution: during development, we let the model update its own memory, build dozens of complex skills for RL experiments, and improve its own learning process based on experiment results. An internal version of M2.7 autonomously optimized a programming scaffold over 100+ rounds — analyzing failure trajectories, modifying code, running evaluations, and deciding to keep or revert — achieving a <strong>30% performance improvement</strong>. On MLE Bench Lite (22 ML competitions), M2.7 achieved a <strong>66.6% medal rate</strong>, second only to Opus-4.6 and GPT-5.4.</p>

</p>

	</span>
</h2>
<p>M2.7 delivers outstanding real-world programming capabilities spanning log analysis, bug troubleshooting, refactoring, code security, and machine learning. Beyond code generation, M2.7 demonstrates strong system-level reasoning — correlating monitoring metrics, conducting trace analysis, verifying root causes in databases, and making SRE-level decisions. Using M2.7, we have reduced live production incident recovery time to <strong>under three minutes</strong> on multiple occasions.</p>
<p>On SWE-Pro, M2.7 achieved <strong>56.22%</strong>, matching GPT-5.3-Codex, with even stronger performance on real-world engineering benchmarks: <strong>SWE Multilingual (76.5)</strong> and <strong>Multi SWE Bench (52.7)</strong>. On <strong>VIBE-Pro (55.6%)</strong>, M2.7 is nearly on par with Opus 4.6. On <strong>Terminal Bench 2 (57.0%)</strong> and <strong>NL2Repo (39.8%)</strong>, M2.7 demonstrates deep understanding of complex engineering systems. M2.7 also supports native <strong>Agent Teams</strong> for multi-agent collaboration with stable role identity and autonomous decision-making.</p>
<p align="center">
  <img width="100%" src="/MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M2.7/resolve/main/figures/agent_teams.gif">
</p>

	<span>
		Professional Work
	</span>
</h2>
<p>M2.7 achieved an <strong>ELO score of 1495</strong> on GDPval-AA (highest among open-weight models), surpassing GPT5.3. It handles Word, Excel, and PPT with high-fidelity multi-round editing, producing editable deliverables. On Toolathon, M2.7 reached <strong>46.3%</strong> accuracy (global top tier), and maintains <strong>97% skill compliance</strong> across 40+ complex skills on MM Claw. On the MM Claw end-to-end benchmark, M2.7 achieved <strong>62.7%</strong>, close to Sonnet 4.6.</p>
